name: EDWARD MARK BIGGS, B.S.C.
motto: "Uneasy rests the head that wears the crown."
description: "Ed" came to the "Sunny Southland" almost a year ago from the frozen hills of the "Northland" wherein is located Dartmouth College. Thus, this is his first and last year at Georgia. He is quite a harum-scarum chap and one never knows what he is going to do next, but can always rest assured that it will be something. "Ed" has made many friends and has won the respect of all, because of his many sterling qualities. Next year "Ed" returns to Dartmouth; and we are not uneasy, for if the world welcomes a man it surrenders to a gentleman.

name: MARIE S. BLACK, B.S.H.E.
motto: "The paths of glory lead but to the grave."
description: Every characteristic of this charming Senior is one to be envied. Attractive, interesting and original, with the added qualities of ability, intelligence, and sparkling wit, Marie's two years at Georgia have been all too short for her many friends. Never allowing anything to interfere with her jolly good times, we wish for her a happy and successful future.

name: MARGARET TAMORA BLANCHARD, A.B.Ed.
motto: "A mind of your own is worth four of those of your friends."
description: The most delightful impression one gets from Margaret is that of Margaret, who stands out behind and above all of her graces. It's a very striking and curious spectacle to behold someone in these days so confidently nonchalant as Margaret and perennial nonchalantness despite her recognition of the numerous things that are wrong with the world is her most distinguishing personal characteristic that draws all to her.

name: FRANK KELS BOLAND, JR., A.B.
motto: "Man is but a worm; he comes along, wiggles, and then some chicken gets him."
description: Many things can be said of the boy to whom this is dedicated, but it is useless. What his fellows think of him and what he has done is told above; likewise what we expect of him can not be written in this short space. Rather let us, as with this book we dream of these days, think of Kels as a friend of our college days, with his head that defied Stetson—full of wit, wisdom and determination.

name: JAMES EMORY BOYD, A.B.
motto: "A taste of knowledge is dissatisfying, but at the same time, inspiring."
description: James Emory seems to have achieved a nice balance in judging the value of things. While making a brilliant record in scholarship, one of which anyone could well be proud, he has at all times kept studies in their proper place, and was never too absorbed in them to take an interest in the other important things of life. His geniality and sincerity have won him many friends who are confident that his future success will equal his past.

name: PAULINE BRACKETT, A.B.Ed.
motto: "All real success comes from reliance on some power higher than human."
description: One does not meet a smile more winsome or a greeting more cheerful than Pauline's. She brings sunshiny thoughts whether at work or at play. She possesses those qualities which endear her to her associates and make her linger in the memory after years of separation. Possessed of a disposition so genial and happy, so energetic and capable, she won a host of friends during her stay at the University.

name: WALKER P. INMAN BRANDON, A.B.
motto: "What I must do is all that concerns me; not what people think!"
clubs: Major 1st Squadron, R. O. T. C.
description: "Ipe" is one of the gifted mortals whose talents lie in all directions. Equestrian, singer, scholar, linguist par excellence, the steadfastness of his admirable, manly character eclipses all of these traits, attractive as they are. His friends acknowledge the privilege of knowing him.

name: JAMES LUTHER BRIDGES, B.S.A.
motto: "Chop your own wood and it will warm you twice."
description: "Red," a congenial, sociable, diligent, quiet, resourceful student and a friend to all. No one has ever found him short of any one of these qualities. During his stay at the University he has made and kept many friends. The world is full of "high-notchers," but it takes a regular fellow to hold the esteem of many as "Red" has done.

name: IDA LEE BROWN
motto: "Success is simply won by the practice of hard work, common sense, self-reliance, and honesty."
description: With a stick of dynamite attached and automatically ignited, as it were, at intervals during her college career, Ida Lee would have dimmed the stars. Meeting obstacles with unruffled poise, keeping her objective ever foremost, she has forged steadily toward her goal, passing it with unobtrusive ability, which is one of her outstanding characteristics. Hers is the nature which makes friends. Her future is destined to be as cheerful as her college days.

name: WILLIAM MARCUS BRYANT
motto: "Professors and classes are my only objection to a college education."
description: Marcus is an exemplar of that philosophy which teaches that man's first obligation is to entertain himself. But in so doing, happily, he scatters joy around him. There are no bright tritenesses to describe him—his turbulent high spirits, his cavalier recklessness, his sheer joy in being alive. Marcus knows the value of the present moment and the wisdom of living it intensely, high-heartedly, perfectly. His is a rare catchment of individuality, more easily remembered than described.

name: ELLEN ADELAIDE BURGER
motto: "Life is what you make it."
description: "None but herself can be her parallel." The author of the above must have had Ellen in view when he penned these words; for this little girl is unparalleled. She minds her own business, keeps her own counsel, and allows trouble to do the same. Ellen is not without her faults and enemies, and what is more, she cares little what people think of her. She is unconscious of her good influence, and is wholesomely sincere.

name: BLYTHE NELL BURNETTE
motto: "Be yourself at all times, and you will be happy."
description: If in your imagination you can picture a person whose face is ever wreathed in smiles, whose countenance is ever radiating happiness, whose voice is ever ringing with joy and fun, and whose personality we all envy, then we have a true picture of Blythe. A worry to her is an unknown thing, but joy and happiness are woven into her nature.

name: ALEXANDER BUSH
motto: "To see life steady and to see it whole."
description: "Alex" raised sufficient hours and Cain in three years to either graduate or be thrown out. His crop of 90's has saved him embarrassment. Marks, a motor car, make this man! As his friends know him, a true friend; as his professors know him, a brilliant scholar; as the Glee Club knows him, a good musician.

name: ERNEST CAMP, JR.
motto: "There's nothing either good or bad, but thinking makes it so."
description: During his four-year sojourn at the University, Ernest has developed worthy capacities into enduring abilities; has worked hard, and enjoyed it. The range of his activities has been extensive, yet his many honors attest his talents as much as his versatility. Never dull, ever sagacious, ofttimes brilliant, this fellow will not soon be forgotten.

name: LOUISE C. CARMICHAEL
motto: "If we always face the sunshine, the shadows will fall behind."
description: We all admire Louise. We think of her as steady, dependable, helpful, and happy withal. We know that she will be successful because of her qualities of leadership. Not seeking to impose her personality on others she is ever alert to serve her friends.

name: RICHARD DAVID CARR, JR.
motto: "Salve, the only panacea!"
description: Carr's discharge from the army and his incarceration at Georgia was heartily concurred in by Uncle Sam on the grounds that his propensity for asking questions might prove less aggravating there than elsewhere. He is above and beyond all lectures. He has questioned his way through every course and was never satisfied with a "gentleman's grade" if he could talk the professor out of a better one.

name: James Rollin Chambliss
motto: "Just roll on, Old Time, I'm coming."
description: Of all those who have roamed our university for the four years past, no one has found richer treasure in its books and personalities, or drunk more deeply of the spirit of the place. But Rollin has not made his mind a mere repository for facts. He questions everything with a healthy skepticism; is as free from illusions, perhaps, as one could safely be. It is difficult—and priceless—to know him.

name: Benjamin Franklin Cheek, Jr.
motto: "No pleasure under the sun Equals that of work well done."
description: Despite his self-alleged misogyny, the fair sex quickly shortened his name to "Ben." He was clever, but honest and sincere, whether as doubter, thinker, speaker, or writer. New ideas was his hobby; he was a modernist both inwardly and outwardly. Sunday was the "Chief's" busiest day, and midnight his noon of thought. Systematic and scientific, he sacrificed energy undissipated by the least personal indulgence. Prominent rather than eminent, radicalism often steered his pen and stirred the campus.

name: Bess Christian
motto: "The clay first broke my heart and then my back; and the back heals not."
description: Just two years ago when Bess came to "ole Georgia," G. S. C.'s loss was the University's gain; and now as she leaves us, the University's loss is the world's gain. With a winning personality and charming manner she casts rays of sunshine into the hearts of all who knew her. With a fun-loving disposition, ever full of joy and happiness, Bess pursued her studies without an undue sacrifice of pleasure. Attractive and full of pep, it is needless to say she was popular among University students of both sexes.

name: Ella Irene Clarke
motto: "Simplicity, sincerity, service."
description: Like a breath of mignonette from a lovely hidden garden, Ella has charmed all whose lives she has touched. A bit of dignity softened by grace and poise and personality. When a word of cheer can be spoken, a ray of happiness brought, an act of service done, Ella needs no reminding. Dependable, steadfast, true, loyal! A good sport. A jolly companion, a conscientious student, a leader in all worthy activities, and a real, honest-to-goodness girl.

name: EUGENE MCRAY CLARK, B.S.C.
motto: "Impossible is a word only to be found in a fool's dictionary."
description: Flip came to us from Stapleton and quickly oriented himself to the glossoligist atmosphere of the University. A love for pleasure, an admiration for the beautiful, and a devotion for his innumerable friends have characterized his eventful career. With alertness of perception he has gathered the fundamentals of commerce without wearing the sossley look of the bibliophile. Flip is of the few who mastered the economic course in three years, notwithstanding the fact that he spent many a moment electrifying maidens' hearts with his perpetual smile.

name: JAMES SELDON COCHRAN, JR., B.S.C.E.
motto: "The foundation of every character is absolute sincerity"
clubs: Vice-President, University of Georgia Student Chapter American Society Civil Engineers, '26-'27
description: After a short sojourn at the Georgia School of Technology, "Scout" came to the University of Georgia in the winter of 1924 to prepare himself for the hardest working profession known to man—civil engineering. He has come out victoriously in more than one struggle toward that most worthy goal. Through his consistent work and tireless efforts he has learned the theory and formulas necessary to build for himself a paved highway to a successful future.

name: RALPH NOBLE CONOLLY, B.S.C.
motto: "Defeat is only for him who accepts it."
description: It was in the fall of 1923 when this distinguished lad registered at the university as local talent from Athens High School. Although he left us during his sophomore year and was enrolled for a few months at Georgia Tech, the call of our old Alma Mater was too strong to resist and we welcomed him back. Ralph has made an excellent record. He has a most pleasing personality and possesses rare qualities of friendship.

name: WILLIE MAE COOK, A.B.J.
motto: "Beauty is truth; truth beauty."
description: Willie Mae is understanding—quite. She is accomplished without being ostentatious, intellectual without being boring, and friendly without being glucosey. And she has the greatest amount of common sense per square inch of anybody I ever knew. With vivacious brown eyes, a welcoming smile, and a kind, lovable—adjectives, adjectives, adjectives! Forget them! Willie Mae is the sort of girl you tell your troubles to.

name: Fred Cureton, B.S.C.
motto: "Silence gives consent."
enrolltime: Fred came to Georgia from Emory, and now ends his senior year.
description: He is the sort of whom one delights to say, I am glad I have known him. He is a "fellow" who minds his own business; is steady-going and never loses sight of the one desired purpose of accomplishing something worthwhile. He is consistent in his habit and his habit is good, so much so that it has been remarked, "Fred is one of the best boys I have known."

name: Dorothy Daniel, A.B.
motto: "Speech is silver, but silence is golden."
description: Yes, she is quiet, but it is not a colorless quiet. She is restful, refreshing—one of the few whose sympathy, intelligence, and whimsical sense of humor, in short, whose charm—is strongly felt with no effort on her part. Unobtrusive when present, decidedly missed when absent, leaving us always wanting to see more of her, to know her better—that is Dorothy.

name: Alvin Holmes David, Ph.G.
motto: "Not how you won or lost, but how you played the game."
description: If the aim of a college is to impart a knowledge of life, breadth of character, charm of manner, and the ability to move easily among a host of friends, it has succeeded admirably in this instance. His knowledge of life and ideals are of the finest type, his character impeccable, his manner such that he numbers his friends by the student directory.

name: James Russel Davis, B.S.C.
motto: "Trust your friends but cut the cards."
description: On a dreary October afternoon a few years back, things suddenly brightened up in Athens for news spread that "Teany Davis" had forsaken Georgia Tech and was now a student at the old University. Well, they might have become brighter, for in a few months his sunny disposition and ready wit made him the friend and companion of all who met him. We regret to see "Teany" leave us; his place will be hard to fill.

name: Harry Fentress Delany
motto: "De gustibus non disputandum."
description: "There lives more faith in honest doubt, believe me, than in half the creeds." Though in pursuit of the truth to the verge of iconoclasm, Harry finds time to sleep. In fact, I think he could have been found in a somnolent attitude at almost any time during his college career. Harry is no doubt something unorthodox. Too often I become enthusiastic with him in denunciation of certain phases of a Biblical legend. But for all of his dissent, he displays an unlimited source of mental energy. What if he is a "bloody radical?"

name: Robert Frank Donaldson, Jr.
motto: "The world is not what it is cracked up to be."
description: This lad, known by several nicknames such as "Little," "Pee Wee," etc., came to the University weighing 110 pounds and measuring five feet four. He has spent four years in Athens, and will leave the classic city weighing 110 pounds and measuring five feet four. His stay here has been taken up with study and frolic. His personality and manner in meeting people will get him somewhere in life.

name: John Henry Dorminy, Jr.
motto: "Unless a man show courage he has no security for preserving any other virtue."
clubs: First Lieutenant, R. O. T. C. Band
description: John entered the University in 1924 with the class of 1928. He determined to "make it" in three years, and has succeeded in graduating with the class of 1927. Loaded with extra studies, he has kept up a good standard and is to be commended in his efforts. You will find him to be an amicable fellow with a clean character and high ideals.

name: Julia Melville Doughty
motto: "Reach high, Mel—and hold."
clubs: President University League of Women Voters, '26, '27
University Representative and Speaker at State Convention League of Women Voters, '26
description: Melville has the rare accomplishment of being able to express herself well either on paper or orally. She is a true friend. The kind of a friend one values as one gets older and sees more of the world.

name: KATHLEEN MERRY DREWRY, B.S.
motto: "One stands revealed by what he strives to attain and seeks to avoid."
description: Mrs. Drewry is an excellent example of the type of person who will not allow anything—not even marriage—to interfere with her search after knowledge. A graduate of Lucy Cobb Institute, Mrs. Drewry, then Kathleen Merry, entered the university as a candidate for the degree, bachelor of science—a degree not generally sought by those looking for snap courses, and one quite infrequently chosen by Co-eds. Her high standing in such courses as chemistry and genetics bespeaks her ability to achieve in a task that appeals to her.

name: PAT DUNN, B.S.C.
motto: "Every man for himself, and the devil take the hindmost."
description: There is little that we can say about Pat for, as we write this, he has turned in neither writeup nor writeup blank. We have been in classes with him, and have found him a quiet, reticent, well-meaning boy. A boy whose friendship is hard to gain evidently, but, from the testimony of mutual friends, one who is bound to you with "hooks of steel" once you have won his confidence and his liking.

name: HYMAN BARNARD ESTROFF, B.S.C.
motto: "Life is man's time, which he may use to his advantage or disadvantage."
description: It is a pleasure to write about a man who has so earnestly striven for all the development college can give. Dominated by the highest ideals, guided by the determination to obtain them, he has thrown his soul into his college work, and laid a firm foundation for future achievement. He is kind and firm; actively busy, and cordially friendly. We know that he will attain what is better than success, the satisfaction of work well done and a life well lived.

name: PEARCE LESTER ELKINS, B.S.A.
motto: "What should a man be but merry?"
description: Here is a man, energetic and industrious, who is willing to do his best and let the rest pass with a smile. "Pistol" is one of the few students who have finished a four-year course in three years. Although very easy to become excited when touched on the ribs, on all other occasions he holds a smooth hand. Neither the tender voice of the feminine sweet, nor the harassing blisters of the canvassers' feet have caused him to sway from the high aspiration of making good in college.

name: JOSEPH FERDINAND FANNING, B.S.C.
motto: "The proverb saith that many a small maketh a great."
description: In his four years in the University, fruitful, while not spectacular, Joe has convinced a large circle of friends of the many excellent qualities embedded in his character. His genial humor and genuine wit, coupled with his unpretentious outlook on life, have drawn to him those who fully appreciate rare companionship and a continuity of sincere friendship. His personality—and this is the most appealing feature of his makeup—is of lasting quality and will ever serve him as an introduction to the best things of life.

name: JOHN WILLIAM FANNING, B.S.A.
motto: "Speak only when speech is required."
clubs: Freshman and Sophomore Alpha Zeta Medals
description: Making possibly every honor in college, J. William now leaves with enough gold to start a jewelry store and enough praise and admiration to become the president of the land. He leaves us the cherished memory of an honest, persistent, intellectually distinguished student and a friend with an admirable disposition, winning personality, and a scholastic record unsurpassed by any in the history of the University.

name: BENJAMIN GOODMAN FEEN, B.S.
motto: "All that I know is that I know nothing."
clubs: student assistant in the chemistry laboratory
description: Ben, with his quiet ways, is a human dynamo. He has shown this in his power to lead and learn at the same time. Coming to the University with the purpose of learning chemistry, he was through his industry and hard work honored with the position of student assistant in the chemistry laboratory. His pleasing personality, his reserved manner, and his keen intellect, have won for him many friends. Ben has all the qualities which go to make up a great somebody and we hope and expect great things of him.

name: WILLIAM ROBINSON FELTON, A.B.
motto: "Look to thyself; leave the rest to God, thy conscience, and the grave."
enrolltime: entered the University with the class of '28
description: "Bill" entered the University with the class of '28 and is to win his degree in three years. His character may best be defined by the terms individuality, sincerity, and frankness. Whatever he has undertaken he has accomplished ably. As yet he is undecided as to where he will take up the study of law. No matter where he pursues his studies he will undoubtedly continue successfully.

name: HENRY HARRIS FITZPATRICK, B.S.C.
motto: "Listen to others; think for yourself."
description: Sturdy, persistent, and reliable are the three words which best describe "Fitz." Although wasting one year of college by going to Emory, he displayed a mark of wisdom and came to the University for his remaining three years. Fitz has made himself very popular here by his personality and willingness to do things. We predict for him a great future in the business world.

name: GEORGE ESLIE FLORENCE, B.S.
motto: "Sincerity."
description: Rare is a soul which wins distinction and prominence, yet does not lose its grasp of genuine values. As a student "Babe" has been scholarly but not pedantic; as an athlete, superb without pretention; as a friend, genuine and unexcelled. His remarkable and outstanding athletic career deterred him not from the fundamentals of life, and was impressive as being an ideal of fair and clean sportsmanship. Babe's quiet and unassuming manner has placed him in the highest esteem of the whole student body.

name: WALTER TILLOU FORBES, B.S.C.
motto: "The strength of your life is measured by the strength of your will."
description: College life—and college athletics—will never fall into disrepute so long as they turn out, once in a blue moon, a man like Walter Forbes. It is difficult to think of him dispassionately—but it will be best to remember him just as he is: plain as an old shoe, clean and courageous, lovably human, and shy.

name: JOHN ROBERT FOWLER, A.B.
motto: "We know what we are, but know not what we may be."
description: To his intimates Bob Fowler gives the impression of an English schoolboy as depicted, perhaps, by Thackeray—reserved, dependable, scholarly, yet athletic and sturdy—characteristics of favorable import for a fortunate future. He is not a man of varied interests, but things finished by his hand bear evidence of excellent workmanship. Bob is a rare kind of a friend—quiet, but congenial and fun-loving—born and bred a gentleman.

name: WILLIAM HOWARD FOY
description: Bill, a son of Sylvester and a graduate of Young Harris College, came here just as ambitious and desirous as any freshman in the class of '27. In accordance with his previous decision he began the study of agriculture, and has been conscientious in his work and efforts throughout his course. "Bill is a likeable chap."

name: ROBERT HOWELL FREEMAN
motto: "He who spends the first half of the day attending to his own business, and the latter half letting other people's business alone, has done a good day's work."
description: When the old "grads" of '27 gather, long years hence, on the campus of their Alma Mater, no one will receive a warmer greeting than genial "Hickey" Freeman. Certainly there is no face better or more affectionately known about the campus and the city. Always lighted with a smile, always ready with a word of good cheer and encouragement, always a-sparkle with boyish fun, Hickey has glorified the heritage of the true Southern gentleman.

name: WILLIAM THOMAS FULLILOVE
motto: "The determination to succeed is the first step toward success."
description: Tom or "kitten," as he is sometimes called in his quiet and polished manner and in spite of the fact that he is from Watkinsville, has made many friends during his four years at Georgia. Possessed of a smooth tongue, good looks, and a winning personality, determined to win always, despite the fact that he does not possess a giant's physique, he weathered the test of four years on the "book field." We predict for him success.

name: MILLARD LUTHER GARNER
motto: "Give every man thine ear, but few thy voice."
description: Here is a man with a dynamic personality. He is admired by his many friends for his pluck and grit. Once your friend he is always your friend. His accomplishments are varied and many.

name: BENNIE MAE GARTRELL
high school: Mary P. Willingham and Georgia State College for Women
motto: "It is written."
description: Good-natured, with a keen sense of humor, a good sport and student, jolly pal and true friend, a rare compound of quality that can not be described, Ben came from Blue Ridge to the University in 1925 by way of Mary P. Willingham and Georgia State College for Women. Georgia was hers the minute she twinkled those eyes and smiled, but with her many admirable traits the friendship became genuine.

name: HOPE HULL GIBSON
motto: "Do unto others as you would have them do unto you."
description: After serving Uncle Sam in the World War, Gibson came to Georgia in 1924 and not only obtained his degree in agriculture, but has also become the proud father of two boys, Bayne and Bobbie. He firmly believes in the old adage: "An ounce of determination, applied to a specific purpose, is worth a ton of genius." We have always found Gibson faithful, honest and charitable, never shirking his duty toward his fellow beings.

name: BESSIE BELLE GILCHRIST
motto: "Aim at the stars—if you hit the barn door."
description: You'd never think to see this little girl strolling leisurely across the campus that she has routed whole classes and their professors. When she lowers her voice to speak, we find ourselves straining our ears to catch the pearls of learning that fall from her lips. Her modest demeanor belies the many honors which have pursued her through college. We find her name enrolled in the lists of athletic, literary, and religious circles.

name: FRANCIS HOWARD GILBERT
motto: "Xenium Vulturnum."
description: "Q-Ball's" four years at the University have been spent with profit to himself and pleasure to others. This is attested by his good scholastic standing and his host of friends. He is going to pursue the study of law at Yale university next fall. After three years there he expects to return to Atlanta, and begin a practice which we hope will be prosperous.

name: DAVID HARRY HARDIN, B.S.
motto: "Trust no one; do the thing yourself."
description: A myriad of men come and go. Some are destined to careers of greatness, others wallow in the dust without effort to rise, not taking advantage of that one opportunity that comes to every one. Be it said of Harry that he has taken advantage of his opportunities, and though not hailed among the present great, he has the ability to do most anything he chooses.

name: JOHN ROLAND HARLOW, B.S.A.
motto: "Before you get between the devil and the deep, be sure you know how to fight fire and swim."
description: Honest John gained his title through honest and persistent effort, through courageous and constant manifestation of high and noble ideals as a political leader. He came to us from Summerville as innocent a Miles Standish type as ever lived. But, after four years of association with the fairer sex, he goes away with many a maiden's heart and the unparalleled ability of approaching the maidens fair in as true and bold a manner as Romeo himself.

name: CHARLES EDWARD HARMAN, A.B.
motto: "Gather ye rosebuds while ye may."
description: Charlie—with his bursts of wit, his bright observations on people and things in general, his refreshingly sentimental outlook on life—is a unique and lovable boy. Anyone who can have moods without inflicting them on others and annoyances without giving annoyance is bound to have a generous and unselfish spirit. In his innate refinement he has never forced himself on people, and consequently his real self was unknown to the majority.

name: JOHN FRANK HARMON, A.B.Ed.
motto: "Let all words be mild, All strifes be reconcil'd, All pains beguil'd."
description: Frank hails from the old red hills of North Georgia, where one's ultimatum is generosity and devoted friendship. He represents all that is ideal in true friendship. Encircling him is an attractive personality that tends to draw and hold one. His chief aim in life seems to be in helping others. May the world accept him in his true capacity, and success and happiness be his.

name: Florence Yvonne Joselove
motto: "A fool and his college are soon parted."
description: A bundle of fun when it's playtime, but when the fun is over, a mind alert and a soul sincere. A circle of friends who hold her dear—she loves, and strongly, nor conceals her hate—a candid nature purely. And then those gray-green eyes—devastation surely.

name: Thomas Gerald Kain
motto: "Be no more than what you are."
description: "Shaky" left his native state, Tennessee, in order to become a Georgia "Cracker," and we feel that this University has profited by this decision, made four years ago. His athletic ability has been a great asset to Georgia, but this is not all; he has shown great sportsmanship in every branch of athletics he has participated in, and made an endeavor to keep athletics on the high plane it should occupy.

name: John Mathews Keith
motto: "Life owes us just what we get out of it."
description: We address not those who know John, but those who have not had the pleasure of becoming intimately acquainted with him. His entire college life is marked by that same quiet determination which overcomes all obstacles and which sooner or later must bring the success and honor so well deserved. A prince of a fellow, and a real friend. What more could be desired?

name: Patrick Joseph Keating
motto: "Life is that invisible unknown that makes man think."
description: Pat came to the University in '24, and through his capacity to absorb knowledge, coupled with honest effort, he has completed the prescribed course in three years. It was the ability to see a definite goal and steel-like determination to keep working toward this end, that has brought to him success. The numerous friends of Pat wish for him all the success and happiness his ability proves him capable of obtaining.

name: LAWRENCE JACKSON KENNEY, B.S.C.
motto: "A man is a failure only when he admits it."
description: Red is one of the finest chaps we know. His scholastic record has been pleasing, and more important, perhaps, his strong personality, character, disposition, and ability have enabled him to get the best the University offers outside the regular book work, and also the friendship of a huge number of friends. Red is one of the few students and college friends who will be remembered—not by any freakish incident connected with him, but by his many strong points.

name: CALEB JEWETT KING, A.B.
motto: "There are more things in heaven and earth, Horatio, than are dreamt of in your philosophy."
description: Gus is one of those rare and exceptional students who have accurately sensed the purpose of collegiate attendance. An intensive and uninterrupted pursuit of knowledge has characterized his entire quadrennium stay at the University. His fixedness of purpose, plus the equipment he has secured from the rich arsenal of his Alma Mater admirably qualify him for the extra-mural activities of life, and make doubly sure his already splendid assurances of success.

name: LOIS COBB LAMPKIN, A.B.Ed.
motto: "To be, not to seem; to do, not to dream."
description: Lois came to Georgia from Mary Baldwin Seminary, where she did her college preparatory work. She is one of our Athens girls of whom the class of '27 may boast. Europe did not have so great a fascination that she did not return to her Alma Mater for her senior year. Her scholarship and enthusiastic support of all college activities prove her ability in varied capacities. Her attractive personality and strong character have won for her many friends. It goes without saying that she will surely be missed next year.

name: LUCY PHELPS LAMPKIN, A.B.Ed.
motto: "Give to the world the best, and the best will come back to you."
description: When Lucy left Mary Baldwin to enter the University, Virginia's loss proved to be Georgia's gain. We have consulted together and we doubt very much if there is anyone who has been at Georgia since she came who has not known and loved her. She has executive ability, scholastic ability, and above all, the ability to make friends among students and faculty. With all the splendid possibilities she possesses, her future should be crowned with success.

name: WILLIAM LEON MOORE, B.S.F.
motto: "Be yourself at all times."
description: W. L. entered in the fall of '22, but dropped out one year in order to get a taste of the cruel world. Coming back in '24, he has become an outstanding student and one worthy of the profession he has chosen. We can do but little in saying that the University has lost a good student and the world has won a friend and a real man.

name: WILLIAM GLENN MORAN, PH.G.
motto: "It's great to be alive, provided you don't die early."
description: Jack, known by his home-town friends as Glenn, comes to the end of his sojourn at the dear old University after a successful stay of only two years. A sterling character, a big heart, and a capacity for tackling any job, has made Jack the man he is—true to his Alma Mater, and an excellent student at all times. We know Jack will not disappoint us.

name: ANNE LOUISE MORRIS, A.B.
motto: "Life and hash are what you make them."
description: It would appear that Anne has gained from her college days a real appreciation of the movies. She leaves us the memory of a loyal and dependable nature and the unusual quality of never having said a mean thing about anyone. There is a certain unconscious sweetness about her and her unobtrusive friendliness will be missed when loud laughs and "heys" across the campus are forgotten. What she puts into the hash is still a mystery, but it is already clear that she has made her life worth living.

name: CICERO DECATUR McCUTCHEN, III., B.S.C.
motto: "Why worry? You will laugh about it when it's all over."
description: C. D. McCutchen, III, hails from Dalton, Georgia. He never has much to say, but what he does say is usually well worth listening to. His one prominent weakness is the fair sex, and we have yet to see the girl who didn't fall for him. The best thing that can be said of anyone can be said of C. D. He has no enemies. Go to it, C. D. We're all for you, and Georgia is proud to have such a son.

name: EDWARD BURNS McDADE, JR.
motto: "Grothi seauton. Magna est veritas, et privalebat."
description: Curriculum to many is a monster which interferes with college life and worries fathers, but to "Mack" it has been an opportunity to add to his store of knowledge. If he shows the same qualities in after life that he has shown in college, his life is sure to be a success.

name: CLOVIS HIGHTOWER McKENZIE
motto: "Women, women, what a blessing!"
description: "Spunk," during his four years at the University, has earned the esteem of his fellow students by his constant application to his work, which, nevertheless, did not prevent him from taking a prominent part in the social activities of the University. We hope that the training that he has received here, coupled with his excellent ability to make and keep friends, will make his life a happy and prosperous one.

name: JOHN THOMAS McKENZIE
motto: "Boldness has genius, power and magic in it."
description: If you have never heard of the peaches of Georgia you have not had the pleasure of meeting John Thomas McKenzie, of Montezuma, Georgia. John came to the University fresh from Emory Academy, where he received his master's degree in political science and oratory, and since being here has been awarded another, master woman hater. You will always recognize John by the smile he continually wears.

name: WILLIAM STEWART McMICHAEL
motto: "No man is stronger than his weakest trait."
description: Fitting words can not be summoned to eulogize Bill and his work at Georgia. A great big ball of optimism, you'll find Bill's disposition, and the longer you know him the better you'll like him. His character is where quality meets. Our best wishes go with Bill into the great university of life and we are most assured that he will make a name for himself on the highway of life. Best of luck, Bill.

name: Callie Terrella McWhirter
motto: "Qui ne risque rien, n'a rien."
description: I swore I'd forget all conventional adjectives when I started this write-up. But no matter how trite good adjectives may become they remain somehow good. Callie was smiling over something she was thinking some years ago when I saw her first. She's been smiling and thinking ever since. She is precisely—Callie. I am reminded seeing her of lilacs, figured window curtains, and a fresh wind out of nowhere in particular. Not that she's fresh in the vulgar sense—oh, no, she's exhilarating.

name: Elnathan Kemper Nelson
motto: "Life is as a blinding snowstorm. No one can see the right path."
description: After spending two years at V. M. I., Kemp decided to take life in a more leisurely fashion. Setting out in the usual Ford to tour the country, his trip was arrested by the classic Athens. "What a wonderful place to start a revolution," he probably thought. In the two years Kemp has been here he has gained a host of friends, and will, he says, always have a tender feeling for Athens and the South.

name: Ernest Driscol Newton
motto: "Procrastination is the thief of time."
description: What's in a name? A lot—when it's a magical monicker like "Newt." No man at the University has spent four years more fruitfully in making friends. Plain and unassuming, brimming with old-fashioned generosity and fun and perennial sunny temper, a shrewd business man, a Beau Brummell first, last, and all the time, a golden-hearted friend and pal—that's "Newt." His orderly habits and thinking have already marked him as a man apart. They will carry him far in the world.

name: Willis Newton Noell
motto: "Truth and sincerity rest equal in friendship."
description: Willis will leave the University this June. His going will be a loss to the campus. He is an industrious student and a zealous seeker of truth. He is among the few students who have learned the true meaning of college life. Willis was a true friend, an honest friend and a friendly friend. We hate to see him go, but our hopes and good wishes go with him into his greater field of service and friendship.

name: CHARLES WAYNE NUITE
motto: "I would stick to the highway, But it's too well trod. Let me go to the by-way, And be near my God."
description: He is a man, and an American. His life work is one of service, and his ambition is to serve. He is a good student, and his friends are many. Sad to relate, he is not a woman hater. That is his philosophy of life.

name: WARRINGTON MAXWELL OLIVER, JR.
description: Max possesses one of those choice and admirable personalities that is indescribable; he is genuine, so true, and is well fitted for any task; that accounts for his being a leading figure in student activities. In 1923 Max displayed his ability on the gridiron but was forced to retire on account of an injury. Max, we wish you the best in life and much happiness.

name: FREDERICK WILLIAM ORR
motto: "As a man thinketh in his heart so is he."
description: "Fritz" Orr is "four-square"—the highest type of manly development physically, mentally, socially, and spiritually. He has won two college degrees and is planning to win more; is prominent in college dramatics and music; and is a bright, attractive, spiritual leader of University students.

name: WILLIAM DANIEL OXFORD
motto: "As a man thinketh in his heart so is he."
description: Bill hails from Sale City. He matriculated at the University in the fall of 1923, with that burning ambition to become a college graduate. He has stuck by his work for four years and will receive his degree in June. "Bill" has been an active student on the campus and has gained many friends. His classmates and associates wish him the success that is sure to come his way.

name: DELACY CRAWFORD PARKER, B.S.
motto: "Resolve to be thyself; and know that he Who finds himself, loses his misery."
description: Though slow in starting any undertaking, once it is entered into, it is a certainty that Delacy will see it through to the end. He is an earnest and consistent worker, and no goal is too high nor too far away for him to attain. His actions, quiet by nature, must be judged as the true foundation of his character, and will most certainly reap their reward.

name: LYMAN PARSONS, B.S.C.
motto: "Let me live in the house by the side of the road and be a friend to man."
description: A congenial disposition and an optimistic spirit are the outstanding characteristics of "Portal," as he is fondly called by his associates at the University. Lyman came to the University in the spring of 1924 and since that time has been a disciple of "Pistol" Jenkins in the department of commerce. During his college career "Portal" has lived up to his philosophy. Quiet, conservative, and a good sport, he has made many friends at the University.

name: ALLEN WILLIAMS POST, A.B.
motto: "Facta verbaque."
description: Graduating in three years, Allen has taken a prominent part in literary and scholastic activities. As Rhodes Scholar, he will study law at Oxford the next three years. In the classroom he talks sense; outside of class he talks.

name: WHITLOW HARRISON POWELL, B.S.C.
motto: "Every man gets what he deserves, but only the successful admit it."
description: The usual recommendation to the world must needs be revised to fit this rare variety of Homo Sapiens, for Whitlow is most decidedly untypical and individualistic. His quiet, unassuming manner and his very admirable habit of never flaunting to the world his many virtues have placed him high in the esteem of all those whose good fortune it is to know him. Whitlow, you are a man worth knowing, but most of all, you are a man.

name: CORNELIA HELEN PROFFITT
motto: "To be, not to seem."
description: Words have never been adequate to express the real worth of a person and it would be a long task to enumerate the lovely qualities Helen possesses. Apparently she is quiet and studious, but one discovers she is full of fun. She is always cheerful, dependable, and energetic. Helen is loved by her friends and we know that wherever she goes she will make many more. Her sympathetic understanding and sincerity endear her to all who know her.

name: HUGH EDGAR RATCLIFFE
motto: "The determination to succeed is the first step to success."
description: Here we have a young man with clean thoughts, a noble ambition, and a desire to make the world a better place in which to live. He has very unassuming manners, and has supreme confidence in his fellowman until they prove that they are unworthy of it. His college friends wish for him success in his chosen field.

name: JULIUS LEONARD RAUZIN
motto: "The flowers that bloom in the spring, tra-la, have nothing to do with—"
description: Like Pandora's box he is an odd jumble of multifarious and adverse qualities—some eccentric, some decadent and others sound, puerile, trite, mystic. An examination of these, although baffling, is intriguing, especially since he assumes a veneer of extravagant volubility—at times to conceal his helplessness; at times his egotism. It would take an adept linguist with multiple adjectives of varying nuances to do this subject justice, over-emphasizing neither his failings nor capabilities.

name: JOEL CONDOR RICHARDSON
motto: "Don't put things off; put them over."
description: With a ready greeting for everyone, and well-prepared recitations for his professors, "Rich" has won an enviable place among both students and faculty. With a readiness for facts and inflexible in their pursuit, he makes a delightful companion for those who love the truth. Of clean-cut stature, and ready flow of wit, he has made a host of friends, especially among the fairer sex.

name: RUTH RITCHIE
motto: "Beauty is truth; truth beauty."
description: "Common sense is good to have, But never let it master you, For then it might deprive you of The foolish things it's fun to do." You've proven to all that you're well blessed with common sense, but more than that you've shown us that you enjoy "the foolish things it's fun to do." Why can't all of us be immune from worry as you are?

name: RUBY YVONNE SAILORS
motto: "That which is worth doing at all is worth doing well."
description: A sterling girl in the true sense of the word, with a heart of gold. She has a great number of friends and to know her better is to appreciate her friendship more. She is never too busy to play a prank, or lend a helping hand. Her generous nature and sympathetic spirit, combined with the above mentioned qualities, go to make her attractive to the extreme.

name: ALBERT DOBBS SAMS
motto: "Meet 'em half way."
description: No one would ever know from "Bert's" manner that he had ever experienced a sorrow or had a care. His happy disposition and open-handed hospitality have won for him scores of friends at the University and throughout the state. Courteous and lovable—a gentleman in every respect.

name: OPAL SATTERFIELD
motto: "Me, too."
description: An outstanding athlete, a hard-working student, and a jolly good sport, that's "Sat." She is possibly the best known co-ed in Athens, always wearing a jolly smile which has won for her many friends. She is one of the few girls to win a major "G" in athletics. As she has done her part in college, we may confidently expect she will do her part in life.

name: William Frederick Schoeffler, Jr.
motto: "He who seeks happiness should not seek education."
description: Bill came south to dodge snow, and he seems to have succeeded, as he has made many warm friends while here. By his sincerity and candor he has convinced us that "Damn Yankee" is two words, and that to be his friend is something to be proud of. This same loyalty to what he thinks best should win Bill a successful place in life.

name: Bruce Talmadge Scoggins
motto: "Your ability and application concentrated on a worth while objective determines your success."
description: Scoggins comes from the city of intellectual giants, Athens, Georgia. He was approached in his sleep by the University ghosts before completing his high school work. Scoggins has figured in military training without becoming militaristic; he has considered the views of the pacifist without growing tearful. His solid smiles shed rays of light on foggy days; his frown stills the rays of sunset lovers. In the whirling juggling world he will stand out as a man free from emotional prejudices.

name: George Bell Scroggs
motto: "Nothing great was ever achieved without enthusiasm."
description: And we heard another gownsman say, "Come and see," and we beheld George Scroggs from Gainesville, Georgia, awaiting the sounding of the intellectual harps. George quickly established himself as one of the foremost academicians and pursued a selective study in his choice field, economics. In the military department, George also distinguished himself as a soldier fit to stand a Waterloo. Through his personal courtesies and jocund personality, multitudes of friends have been acquired.

name: Tom Hill Selman
motto: "Don't condemn a man until you've looked deeper than the surface."
description: Not long since, "Buckle" had a date with his girl, and she told him that she had a dream the night previous, stating it in this manner: "I dreamed that I proposed to the handsomest young man in the world!" Buckle inquires: "Well, what did I say?" We're not insinuating, however, that Buckle is stuck on himself, but under such circumstances as the one above mentioned he will stand up for himself. If personality has anything to do with success he is already a success.

name: WALTER EDWIN SEWELL
motto: "A winner never quits and a quitter never wins."
clubs: Graduate, Summa Cum Laude and Valedictorian, '25
description: "Doc" has conquered three degrees in six years, shattering the highest honors each year as the wintry blasts of frozen Russia shattered Napoleon's army like winter's withered leaves.

name: KIRK SHEPARD
motto: "To thine own self be true and it must follow, as the night the day, thou canst not then be false to any man."
description: Kirk came to Georgia, a boy—wondering. He came to play, I think, and work perchance when play was done, and live a lot, and learn. And playing, working, living, learning, he comes out after four years, not greatly changed, perhaps, but broader, deeper, more secure in his affection for the life that's man's. Patient, unassuming, quiet is his nature.

name: JAMES CLARENCE SHEPPARD
motto: "Life is what you make it."
description: The quiet habits and unassuming personality of "Fat" Sheppard have won for him many friends in the ranks of the students and among the faculty. "Shep" has not made a whirl-wind parade of his stay in college, but he has gone about his duties quietly and sincerely; consequently he has gotten that which he came for—added information and a broader conception of life. We prophesy such will be his tactics in life. His friends wish him the good fortune and success he is sure to win.

name: CECIL WYMAN SHERLOCK
motto: "Youth is a blunder; manhood is a struggle; old age a regret."
description: Hailing from Augusta but at home wherever he hangs his hat, "Ikey" is to leave us this year in person but not in spirit. After having graduated from the School of Commerce in three years he came back for his A. M., which is quite a job in one year's time, considering that he plays both football and baseball.

name: DONALD GREGORY SULLIVAN
motto: "A man's a man for a' that."
description: Methinks this Irishman has disturbed me too much with his sinister sophistry in the past few years. No doubt he should have been given the opportunity to vent his chicanery with the ancients and lead a school of gullible youths in a cool, sequestered grove, where he could retire, as is his wont, and give his vagaries over shepherds and shepherdesses their full release. This man is guilty of neologisms, a license permitted only to masters. I think he is not a master of any sort; he is only a mountebank.

name: CARL ALEXANDER SUTKER
motto: "He is well paid that is well satisfied."
description: He leaves the University with an enviable record, an unblemished character, the most sanguine expectations. He leaves behind him a host of friends among whom his influence will always be felt because of his frankness and magnanimity. It can truly be said "a kinder gentleman treads not the earth." He speeds forth in an unsettled universe to affect the realization of those shining qualities with which he is so lavishly endowed.

name: WILLIAM ABNER SUTTON
motto: "Smile and the world smiles with you; cry and you cry alone."
description: Willie has blazed his way through the University with his good humor, enthusiasm and everlasting love to make good in after life. He graduates, leaving behind him hundreds of friends as milestones along the way. Men may come and men may go, but Willie will go on, forever inspiring friends to higher and nobler deeds while he makes his mark on the everlasting pillar of eternity, the hearts of those who know him best.

name: ROBERT EARL TANNER
motto: "War is hell and so is Botany."
description: The cognomen of "Goat" was bequeathed to Tanner by the elder "Goat" on the completion of his outstanding college career. "Goat" has lived a collegiate life that is admirable from every standpoint. He is likable, and is faithful in duty and shirks no circumstance whatever the outcome, provided he is right. He possesses the rugged honesty typical of the mountain people. "Goat" is true in friendship and devoted to his sleep.

name: Gladys Tappan
motto: "Do unto others as you would have others do unto you."
description: Gladys began her scholastic career by graduating at G. S. C. W. Being ambitious she included the University of California and Cornell in her places of study. After doing extension work in Alabama for several years, she came back to Georgia, and we are the gainers since she selected the University as her Alma Mater. Moreover, we predict that she is going yet higher, for with a combination of ambition, ability, and perseverance she will certainly make more than a mark in the world.

name: Coy Hudson Temples
motto: "It's great to be shot at sunrise, if you can afford it."
description: "Red" has very successfully completed a two-year course in three years, being forced to withdraw from school for one year. Not at all daunted he re-entered, passed his courses without retracing a single step. "Red" has carried himself throughout his college career in a manner fitting a Georgia man. No truer friend could be found, and no better companion; his hosts of friends wish for him only the best things in life.

name: Ernest Henry Thomas
motto: "Think, think, think; then speak."
description: Ernest first came to the University in the fall of '19. After finishing his freshman year he decided life was too difficult alone, so he secured a wife and settled down to farming near Martin. Realizing in the fall of '24 that his education was not complete, he returned to the University and resumed the pursuit of his studies. We haven't room to tell you all of this big-hearted gentleman's manly qualities, but we are willing to shout that he is every inch a man.

name: Hue Thomas, Jr.
motto: "If 't were done, 'tis best 't were done quickly." "The man that loves and laughs must surely do well."
clubs: Student Chapter, American Society Civil Engineers
description: Shakespeare, with his piercing eye must have been looking down through the ages with one eye fixed on the engineering department and the other one on Hue Thomas, Jr., for Hue, with all his love for dances and dates (pronounced "dences and detes" after true Savannah fashion), is an executive capable of engineering anything from a problem in surveying to a fraternity.

name: Jesse Bright Thompson
motto: "Act natural."
description: "Goldie" is one of the few students who, once they are gone from their school, will yet leave something lasting, though intangible; something affectionately entangled with the spirit of the place. In an official way, of course, he will be remembered as a prominent leader on the campus, an able student, business manager of the PANDORA, and so on. But he will best be remembered as "Goldie"—a bundle of generous impulses fronted by a million-dollar smile.

name: Mary Elizabeth Tillman
motto: "The greatest happiness comes through the conscious pursuit of a worthy purpose."
description: Since Mary has given us but two brief years, our loss is more poignant on knowing her, for only then can we realize what we have missed. Willingness personified—to ask of her means to receive—and always willingly; what a pleasure to have her around. Life must hold much for you, and "may you reap its golden harvest to the full."

name: Ralph Harmon Tolbert
motto: "It's all Greek to me."
description: Ralph entered Georgia four years ago, actuated by a desire "to inquire into the causes of things." Equipped with a Ford to serve as a conveyance between this city and his home at Hull, Georgia, he started out; after three years of combined education and travel, he gave up his Ford and moved into Candler Hall. The cultural value of a year's stay in Candler Hall, added to the training derived from four years of honest application, makes success with Ralph merely a matter of time.

name: Millard Leon Treadwell
motto: "Success is yours if you can make the stride."
description: A soldier, a business manager, a student and a ladies' man. Versed in the equations of chemistry and with a superior knowledge of psychology, "Lavenski" has been able to make a name for himself in the scholastic world. With a winning personality and an ever ready willingness to help a friend in need, he has gained many friends and made his many political aspirations come true.

name: H. SAPELO TREANOR
motto: "Duty is the sublimest word in the English language."
description: One of the mature women taking advantage of the opportunities for self-improvement which the University offers, Miss Treanor, while not taking great part in the college activities, has yet secured great benefit from her sojourn here. To those who know her, she reveals a quiet, sensitive, deep-thinking spirit which wells up from hidden depths to show her true worth to her close friends.

name: WILLIAM T. TUCKER
motto: "Never say die."
description: His chance may be dim, but it's nothing to him; he faces his task with a fight; he'll never give in for he's bound to win; he's at it with all his might. Fighting means spirit and vim; he's turning a trick while others licked—right on with his fight to the welcome height; he's there when there's something to be done.

name: HOYT JAMES BARRY TURNER
motto: "Contentment is death to ambition."
description: Turner is one of those unassuming fellows who believes in getting "on top" by one's own merits. He is not an "I'll try" but an "I will" type. He has won his "G" in both basket-ball and track and is the holder of the Southern pole-vaulting record. In addition his great speed on the court won the distinctive title of "Flash" for him. After seeing him in action one can readily see how he gained this title.

name: MARIE TURNIPSEED
motto: "Live to learn and learn to live."
description: It is a tradition that women with brains are entitled to little else. Even so, every once in a while some clever little cherub upsets the dope by proving the exception that makes the rule. Marie is blissfully ignorant of this tradition; wouldn't know a cherub if she should meet one, and still thinks the a, b, c's stand for the alphabet instead of the trio of virtues which belong to her—ability, beauty and charm.

name: HERMAN ANDERSON USHER, A.B.
motto: "It's mine. Let me live it."
description: This strange little parcel of paradoxes has long perplexed us. He has done more than perplex us. He has overwhelmed us with astonishment. In what way he has astonished us most I will not reveal, but in passing our judgment upon this baffling character, we attribute a large portion of the perspicuity and mental acumen in the University to him. He has been a student without confining himself, and has merited the adjective "brilliant" for his display of sparkling intelligence.

name: ASHTON GRAY VARNEDOE, B.S.A.
motto: "Enjoyment is the only reason for living, which may mean many things."
description: Speaking in an editorial vein, we would like to say that our college life has been full of adventure, both mental and physical, full of wonderful companionships, quickening contacts, and influences that have made us really live. In years to come when this book is taken from some dusty shelf and we gaze upon our picture, for that is always the main attraction for any of us, in our annual, we hope that we will have kept our youth of spirit.

name: CORNELIUS VASON, JR., B.S.C.
motto: "A gentleman is not always amorous, but he is always grateful."
description: Neil has made an excellent record at the University, and as to his interest in outside activities, the honors enumerated above speak for themselves. We will best remember him by two outstanding characteristics—his luck, and his ease of making friendships. As to the latter quality we have found him to be a true and loyal friend.

name: DENZIL WARREN WAITS, PH.G.
motto: "Mind your own business."
description: Here is a man who is not in the least conspicuous, but yet is distinctive. It would be hard to find a more conscientious student and it can never be said that he shirks his responsibilities. "D," as he is known to his friends, plans to enter medical school in the fall and we are sure that he will some day be a successful M.D., because he loves the work and is untiring in his efforts to attain his high and noble ambition.

name: GORDON STAPLETON WALDEN, B.S.A.
motto: "The only way to have friends is to be one."
description: We submit for your approval a student who came to us from Riverside Military Academy, and won a place in the esteem of faculty and students that would be an honor to any man. So, Gordon, keep that versatile personality, and as you have a place in the hearts of many here, so will you win success in your undertakings. Wherever we meet, your handclasp will be reminiscent of days both good and bad here where we met.

name: THOMAS BYNE WALKER, A.B.
motto: "Who loves not knowledge?"
description: Byne entered Georgia in the fall of '23. He is an indefatigable worker, and has made an enviable scholastic record during his four years. Notwithstanding the fact that he has applied himself diligently to his studies, he is in no sense of the word a grind and has participated to a sane degree in the social life of the University. Since someone has said that "genius is the capacity for hard work," we are confident that Byne will be heard from in the future.

name: JOHN WOOLFOLK WALKER, JR., A.B.J.
motto: "Everything is in being able to say, 'I have done my best.'"
description: John came to the University from Richmond Academy in the fall of '24. He has a natural gift of drawing friends to him and holding them there in the strongest bond of affection. He is a conscientious worker; and although finishing in three years we find him far above the average in his scholastic standing. Our best wishes go with you as you leave us now, and may your abilities have their just reward in the future.

name: TRAUL GARLAND WALTERS, B.S.A.
motto: "To travel far and hopefully is better than to arrive."
description: Walters hails from Franklin county, a man with clean thoughts and noble purpose. His road to success has not been without sacrifice and struggle, yet Walters has accomplished four years' work in three years and two summer schools. His hobby is serving others. In preparing himself to teach he has not missed his calling. Walters, we all love you and wish you Godspeed.

name: James Colton Weeks
motto: "To thine own self be true; thou can'st not then be false to any man."
description: A matriculate of the Citadel, Teddy served in the army during the war and afterward taught several years before entering the University last fall. As a student of books, he stood high; as student of life he stood higher. Cordial in his likes and dislikes, yet displaying a genial fairmindedness, he leaves with the liking of those who know him; with the high esteem of those who knew him well.

name: Minnie Evelyn Wehunt
motto: "Give to the world the best you have, and the best will come back to you."
description: Minnie enters her tasks whole-heartedly and always comes out victorious. While here she has proven herself to be a good student. She possesses the qualities which enable her to make friends and keep them. Her smile is always helpful, for it is but the outward sign of a soul that is sincere and strong. With all her splendid possibilities her future should be crowned with success.

name: Henry Hoke West
motto: "It's better to be broke and out of a job, than never to have gone to college at all."
description: "Joe Ben," as he is affectionately known on the campus, is one of those fellows who, believing in himself and his own happiness, doesn't try to attend to everyone's business. His love for fun, and his interest in Candler Hall "bull sessions" have won for him a host of friends. His loyalty to his friends, his high moral ideals, and his good nature make him a fellow we hate to lose.

name: Virginia Wilbanks
motto: "Apply your ability and success is yours for the asking."
description: "Out of the hills of Habersham," through the halls of G. S. C. W., came Virginia. During her year at Georgia she has earnestly studied home economics, her chosen profession. How well is attested by the fact that for the entire time she has been one of the leaders of her class. Beneath an exterior of calm and sweetness we ever so often catch a glimpse of a steadfast purpose and a determination to accomplish some good.

name: HUGO CLAUDE WILLIAMS, B.S.A.
motto: "It isn't how you look, nor what others think; it's what you are that really counts."
description: Claude, since he has been in college has proven himself to be a man. He has worked hard not only in the classroom but also in student activities. He has always coupled common sense with his work and when he sets out for a goal it is his. He is a persistent worker, a clear thinker, a true friend, and a real man.

name: JOHN WELDON WILLIAMS, A.B.Ed.
motto: "May life, for me, be service to those with whom I live."
description: Professor Williams has spent a long life building up bigger and better schools. Back when the co-ed boy had an equal chance with the old maid at the State Normal School, "Bill" left the institution determined to live up to the honors conferred upon him. His worth and skill has been demonstrated in the accrediting of high schools at Winterville, Braselton, and Statham, the latter being his greatest work. While doing this great constructive work, he has had the grit by extra work and hard study, to earn a college degree.

name: HATTIE LAURA WILSON, B.S.H.E.
motto: "Whatever is worth doing at all is worth doing well."
description: Upon entering the University, Hattie Laura hitched her wagon to the star of academic learning with characteristic high attainment. That her scholarship characterized her entire record is shown by the fact that she was chosen a member of both Alpha Mu and Phi Kappa Phi. Perhaps her outstanding gift lies in the realm of the aesthetic and is shown in her creative ability in fashioning beautiful things with her hands.

name: BOWLING COX YATES, A.B.
motto: "Not failure, but low aim, is crime."
description: B. C. entered Oglethorpe University at the age of fifteen, and remained there his freshman and sophomore years. He made the Debating Council his freshman year and was manager of the college orchestra there, in addition to the pursuance of his forensic activities. Since coming to Georgia, B. C. has devoted most of his time to scholastic work. In a "session" or in conversation, B. C.'s flowing humor, wit and seemingly iconoclastic attitude, make his society delightful.

name: Lee Holmes Hilton
motto: "Think well what you speak, but speak not all you think."
enrolltime: five-year course, two years of A.B., three of LL.B.
description: Success makes inferior people conceited. L. H. bears his success as lightly and as naturally as those who are accustomed to it are wont to do. This year he gathers his reward for five years of successful pursuit of an education. His five-year course, two years of A.B., three of LL.B., was taken in his stride toward success. His pleasing personality has won him success and staunch friends while here. The concentration of his expression reflects a busy and alert mind, the keynote to his personality.

name: Robert Lawton Le Sueur
motto: "Sans wine, sans song, sans singer and sans end!"
description: Lawton was licensed to practice law in Georgia in 1921 at the tender age of seventeen. After receiving his degree from the Atlanta Law School, he decided to seek new fields for the study of law. With ambition, a splendid mind, and the courage to persevere, Lawton should command success in his legal practice. We shall confidently watch his progress and expect him to reflect great credit upon the school.

name: Edward Lloyd Lewis
motto: "May the best man win."
description: Lloyd hails from Greensboro, and although we have never been able to distinguish this spot on the map, we take his word for it, for Lloyd is truthful at all times. His truthfulness extends to the classroom, where, if his opinion be asked on a knotty problem of law, his idea is generally the true version. We have here an observer on life, who, at the same time is constantly striving to make his own life worth while and well ordered.

name: Alexis Abraham Marshall
motto: "Don't count duty's cost."

name: John Linton Overstreet
high school: Central High School of Washington, D. C.
motto: "Chiefly the mould of a man's fortune is in his own hands."
description: Seldom does a man attract such a host of friends, but his loyalty, which has never been found wanting, has gained for him untold popularity. His independence of character will assert itself in later life, as it has here, and bring him success in his chosen profession.

name: Robert Anthony Rainey
motto: "The pursuit of a vague, intangible thing called 'Happiness' and never caught."
description: Underneath a quiet, dignified and reserved exterior, you find the characteristics that account for his popularity and success. He is sincere, broad-minded, and dependable. He goes into any undertaking with vigor and does not quit until he has thoroughly accomplished what he set out to do.

name: Dean Johnston Ratliffe
motto: "Life is too serious to be taken seriously."
clubs: Editor-in-Chief, Georgia Cracker, first and second terms, 1926-1927
description: Dean's influence will be keenly missed. A friend and companion of the students and faculty alike. Respected and admired by all for his intelligence, gentleness, and ready wit. A true friend and apt student, a leader, a humorist, and a thinker.

name: William Edward Robinson, Jr.
motto: "You are not what you think you are; but what you think, you are."
description: Amiable, debonair, courteous, thoughtful—here we have a gentleman to the manor born—a prince of friends—a student ever in the forefront of the most vivifying movements of Georgia. His every acquaintance is proud to claim him as a friend, which fact is attested in ample fashion by his evident popularity.

name: Clyde Henry Sauls
motto: "It is not who won or lost, but how you played the game."
description: "Curly," realizing the necessity for higher learning in the legal profession, decided to return to us and complete the requirements for his degree. Besides taking his studies seriously, he decided that a "pal" could cheer and encourage him. Acting upon the above conclusion he brought him a companion and mate back after the holidays and is overcoming the obstacles with determination and thoroughness.

name: Thomas Averett Taylor
motto: "Don't envy success—be successful."
description: Averett has served his time in the law school and during his stay has established himself as a student and a leader in college activities, and bids fair to be a leader among men. Look out, Clarence Darrow, Averett will soon be able to enter the law practice, hitting on all eight, and no matter what besets him, nothing will stop him, for he is determined to make a lawyer.

name: Noel Branch Wright, Jr.
motto: "Always keep something up your sleeve for yourself."
description: After spending three winters at Vanderbilt University, where he received his academic training, "Noel" became sensible and dropped in on us. He entered the Lumpkin Law School here three years ago. These years have been profitably spent; his scholastic standing and many friends testify that. "Noel" expects to practice law in Savannah after graduating and we are expecting great things from him.

name: Col. Z. Pepporious Gidley
motto: "Where are you going, my pretty maid?"
clubs: Seventh Assistant Stage Manager, Palace Theater
Third Annual Students' Welfare Y. M. C. A. Religious Conference
description: "Dizzy" Gidley came to us from Knox Institute; we never learned why he left. During his four years at the Onionversity he has carried away numerous honors, shirts, collars and cigarettes. As a child he always had taking ways—he has never got away from them. His favorite flower is the pansy, because it grows in beds. He will go far in life—unless he is caught.
